name: stix2 (STIX Threat Intelligence Python API)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3); OASIS CTI Technical Committee

auth.notes: TAXII 2.x server auth for distribution; local use requires no auth

performance.notes: JSON serialization/deserialization; TAXII queries are network-bound

agent_readiness.notes: Python API for STIX 2.x (Structured Threat Intelligence eXpression). import stix2. indicator = stix2.Indicator(pattern="[file:name = 'malware.exe']", pattern_type='stix', labels=['malicious-activity']). bundle = stix2.Bundle(objects=[indicator]). bundle.serialize(). Filter: fs = stix2.FileSystemStore('./stix-data'). fs.query([Filter('type', '=', 'indicator')]). taxii2client for TAXII distribution. Relationships: stix2.Relationship(relationship_type='indicates'). MITRE ATT&CK: use mitreattack-python. Stores: MemoryStore, FileSystemStore, CompositeStore.

gotchas: STIX 2.0 vs 2.1 compatibility — check which version TAXII server supports
Strict schema validation can fail on malformed STIX from third-party sources
TAXII 2.x is separate package (taxii2-client) from stix2
MITRE ATT&CK requires separate mitreattack-python package
best_when: Creating and validating STIX threat intelligence objects
Ingesting STIX bundles from threat feeds
avoid_when: Proprietary threat intel formats — STIX is standards-based only
summary: stix2 is the Python API for STIX 2.x threat intelligence. stix2.Indicator(pattern="[file:name='malware.exe']"...) creates STIX objects. Bundle serialization. MemoryStore/FileSystemStore for local collections. taxii2-client for TAXII 2.x distribution. Relationships between STIX objects. Use mitreattack-python for ATT&CK data. OASIS standard. Used by MISP, OpenCTI, threat intel sharing platforms. BSD-3.
